text.title: RAG Pipeline: Chunking, Retrieval, and Reranking
text.summary: Core pipeline stages with concrete configuration rules: chunking strategy selection, retrieval strategy selection, and the mandatory reranking pattern.

section.title: Chunking Strategies

rule.statement: Use recursive chunking with 256-512 token chunks and 10-20% overlap as the default; switch to semantic or structure-aware chunking for Markdown/HTML/code.
rule.rationale: Chunk size trades off context preservation vs retrieval precision. 256-512 tokens balances semantic completeness with specificity. Overlap prevents context loss at boundaries.

section.p: Strategy guide: Fixed-size → simple documents. Recursive → most applications (default). Semantic → knowledge bases and technical docs. Structure-aware → Markdown, HTML, code (preserves structure). LLM-based → high-value documents only (expensive).

antipattern.description: Using fixed chunk sizes without overlap for continuous prose documents.
antipattern.reason: Sentences split at chunk boundaries lose context. A question whose answer spans a boundary returns a chunk containing only half the evidence.

section.title: Retrieval Strategies
section.p: Vector search → general Q&A. Hybrid (BM25 + vector) → technical queries with exact names and acronyms. Multi-query → complex questions with multiple sub-intents. Hierarchical → large document sets. Auto-merging → context-dependent answers.

rule.statement: Use hybrid search (BM25 + vector) for production RAG over technical documentation; pure vector search misses exact entity names and domain acronyms.
rule.rationale: Vector search handles semantic similarity but fails on exact matches for rare terms. Hybrid consistently outperforms pure vector on technical corpora.

section.title: Mandatory Reranking

rule.statement: Always rerank: retrieve broad (top-20) then rerank to top-5 using a cross-encoder. This pattern outperforms top-5 direct retrieval by 15-20% precision.
rule.rationale: ANN search retrieves by approximate distance; cross-encoder reranking uses full attention to compute precise relevance. The two-stage approach maximizes both recall and precision.

section.reference: RAG generation prompt with source-only constraint and citation format

section.title: Embedding Model Selection
section.p: Model guide: text-embedding-3-small (1536 dims, 8191 ctx) → cost-efficient prototyping. text-embedding-3-large → production OpenAI. voyage-3 (1024 dims, 32K ctx) → long documents, best retrieval recall. bge-large-en-v1.5 → local/self-hosted. all-MiniLM-L6-v2 (384 dims) → fast lightweight.

rule.statement: Cache embeddings: store content hashes and skip re-embedding unchanged chunks on re-ingestion.
rule.rationale: Re-embedding unchanged documents on every ingestion is the dominant cost for incrementally updated corpora. Content hashing reduces embedding cost to delta-only.
